statement: Submitted source for registry review: Please consider covering agent-memory / agent preflight-memory benchmarks as a CodeSOTA area, including Agent Memory Ben

extractedValue.notes:
Please consider covering agent-memory / agent preflight-memory benchmarks as a CodeSOTA area, including Agent Memory Benchmark (AMB) and Audrey's public memory benchmark artifacts.

Audrey project: https://github.com/Evilander/Audrey
Public report with graphs: https://huggingface.co/spaces/Evilander/audrey-memory-benchmark-report
Raw artifacts dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Evilander/audrey-memory-benchmark-artifacts
AMB provider request: https://github.com/vectorize-io/agent-memory-benchmark/issues/11

Important boundary: this is not a request to add Audrey as a LoCoMo/AMB score yet. The current Audrey artifacts are local deterministic regression/performance evidence. We opened the AMB route so Audrey can be evaluated through the official harness before any leaderboard claim. The broader coverage request is for agent memory systems that prevent repeated agent failures, preserve valid-time beliefs, handle updates/deletes/contradictions, and expose evidence-backed preflight decisions.
extractedValue.title: Please consider covering agent-memory / agent preflight-memory benchmarks as a CodeSOTA area, including Agent Memory Ben

statement: Audrey publishes a local memory engine with recall, consolidation, contradiction handling, lifecycle checks, benchmark gates, and public local benchmark artifacts.
Audrey memory benchmark report is public supporting evidence for deterministic local regression and performance behavior, not an official AMB leaderboard result.
Audrey memory benchmark artifacts dataset is public raw evidence for review and reproduction before any leaderboard claim.
Audrey has an open provider request for AMB evaluation; CodeSOTA should wait for an official AMB run before adding any Audrey AMB score.

statement: Submitted source for registry review: Hi, I was going through your PaddleOCR-VL report and had a quick question about the claimed 167× cost reduction via self

extractedValue.notes:
Hi, I was going through your PaddleOCR-VL report and had a quick question about the claimed 167× cost reduction via self-hosting.

Could you share more details on how this number was derived? Specifically:

* GPU type used
* Inference setup (batch size, pipeline, etc.)
* Throughput assumptions (pages/sec)
* Cost model (e.g., cloud pricing vs. hardware amortization)

From my own tests, I’m getting a similar pages/sec rate as reported in the paper, but the cost reduction factor seems quite different from what I’d expect.

Would really appreciate any clarification - I might be missing something in the assumptions.

Thanks!
extractedValue.title: Hi, I was going through your PaddleOCR-VL report and had a quick question about the claimed 167× cost reduction via self

extractedValue.notes: The paper uses FID (Fréchet Inception Distance) as the primary metric for image generation quality. In the provided schema, 'pass@1' is used as a placeholder for the FID score as it is the primary performance metric reported in the tables.
extractedValue.paper_title: Representation Fréchet Loss for Visual Generation

extractedValue.notes: The paper introduces MedMemoryBench. Results are extracted from Table 3 (Main results) under the 'Mixed' setting for the 'Avg.' column. The dataset ID was mapped from the provided list.
extractedValue.paper_title: MedMemoryBench: Benchmarking Agent Memory in Personalized Healthcare

extractedValue.notes: remove this
Please reach out at harshitajain@smallest.ai for API credits in case you run any internal benchmarks too before listing!

The paper reports results for 10 simulation tasks. The values provided in the results section are representative relative L2 distance scores (lower is better) for specific tasks (e.g., sus-16-034_sim-TChiWZ and sus-16-047_sim-T5Wg_highHT) as reported in Table 2. The 'pass@1' metric in the JSON schema is used here to represent the performance metric reported in the paper.
extractedValue.paper_title: Collider-Bench: Benchmarking AI Agents with Particle Physics Analysis Reproduction
extractedValue.superseded_reason: Initial agent extraction flattened per-task relative-L2 cells into pass@1. Replaced with reviewed aggregate Collider-Bench metrics.
